3.happening: Start an empty list of tree nodes.
3.why: We create node objects as we go.

4.happening: Start a lookup from node id to node object.

6.why: Each new node gets the next id.
6.invariant: next_id[0] is the next free id.

7.happening: Build the tree from the full preorder and inorder arrays.

8.happening: Serialize the finished tree into level-order form.

9.why: The tree is built and serialized.

12.happening: This sub-array is empty — no node here.
12.why: An empty slice means a missing child.

12.happening: This sub-array has {len(preorder)} value(s) — make its root.
12.why: A non-empty slice has a subtree root.

13.happening: Return None — this child is absent.
13.why: Nothing to build for empty input.

14.happening: The first preorder value ({preorder[0]}) is this subtree's root.
14.why: Preorder lists a subtree's root first.

15.happening: Take the next free node id ({next_id[0]}).
15.why: Each node gets a unique id.

16.why: The next node gets the following id.

17.happening: Create the root node with value {root_value}.
17.why: A new tree node pops in.
17.invariant: A fresh node has no children.

18.happening: Add the new node to the tree.

19.why: Children attach to it by id.

21.why: Every node but the root links to a parent.

21.happening: This is the overall root — no parent.

22.happening: It is the parent's LEFT child.
22.why: The parent asked for its left subtree.

22.happening: It is the parent's RIGHT child.
22.why: The parent asked for its right subtree.

23.happening: Attach node {node_id} as node {parent_id}'s left child.
23.why: The edge appears in the tree.
23.invariant: The parent's left link points here.

25.happening: Attach node {node_id} as node {parent_id}'s right child.
25.why: The edge appears in the tree.
25.invariant: The parent's right link points here.

26.happening: Linear-scan inorder to find the root — this is the O(n) cost.
26.why: The brute force has no index map, so it searches.
26.invariant: root_index is where the root sits.

27.happening: Everything before the root is the left subtree.
27.why: In inorder, left-subtree values precede the root.

28.happening: Copy the left subtree's preorder values into a new slice.
28.why: The recursive call needs just those values.
28.invariant: Slicing copies — the O(n^2) space cost.

29.happening: Copy the left subtree's inorder values into a new slice.
29.why: Its inorder segment precedes the root.

30.happening: Recursively build the left subtree from those slices.
30.why: It attaches under this node as the left child.

31.happening: Copy the right subtree's preorder values.
31.why: They follow the left block in preorder.

32.happening: Copy the right subtree's inorder values.
32.why: Its inorder segment follows the root.

33.why: It attaches under this node as the right child.

34.happening: Both subtrees built — mark node {node_id} done.

35.why: The parent records it as a child.

38.why: We search inorder from the front.

39.happening: Still scanning inorder for the root.
39.why: We advance until we find it.

39.happening: Reached the end of the scan.

40.why: This position splits left from right.

40.happening: Position {i} is not the root — keep scanning.

41.happening: Return this index ({i}) as the root's position.
41.why: It tells build where to split inorder.

42.happening: Not this one — move to the next element.
42.why: Linear search steps one at a time.

45.why: We serialize the tree to return it.

46.happening: Start a queue to walk the tree breadth-first.

47.happening: The tree is non-empty — seed with the root.
47.why: Level order starts at the root.
An empty tree serializes to empty.

50.happening: Take the next id from the front.
50.why: BFS emits the earliest-queued id first.

51.why: Level order records holes as null.

52.happening: Emit null for the missing child.

58.why: It is not part of the answer.

init.happening: Setting up empty structures for the build.
init.why: Nodes are created as we recurse.

recurse.happening: Descending to build a subtree from its slices.
recurse.why: Each call builds one root, then its children.
recurse.invariant: The call stack traces the path.

check.happening: Testing a slice, a child side, or a scan position.
check.why: Empty slices make no node; the scan finds the root.

move.happening: Returning from a completed subtree or a finished scan.
move.why: Results hand back to the caller.

update.happening: Creating a node, linking it, or slicing sub-arrays.
update.why: This is where the tree grows — and where slicing copies.
update.invariant: State, links, and slices reflect the work.

return.happening: Serializing and returning the finished tree.

loop.happening: Walking the tree breadth-first to serialize it.
loop.why: Level order is a BFS emit.
